title: Porta Nigra (the Black Gate)
excerpt: The Black Gate, Trier's landmark and the best-preserved Roman city gate north of the Alps.

title: Trier Cathedral (Dom St. Peter)
excerpt: Germany's oldest bishop's church. Home to the Holy Robe, the cathedral treasury, and Romanesque mass right on the Hauptmarkt (the main market square).

title: Liebfrauenkirche (Church of Our Lady)
excerpt: One of the oldest Gothic churches in Germany, right next to the cathedral.

title: Konstantinbasilika (Basilica of Constantine)
excerpt: The ancient throne hall of Emperor Constantine, today the city's main Protestant church. A 27 m high hall with no internal supports.

title: Imperial Baths (Kaiserthermen)
excerpt: Monumental Roman bath ruins. Visitors can explore apses, hypocaust channels, and the underground service tunnels.

title: Barbara Baths (Barbarathermen)
excerpt: The older and larger sibling of the Imperial Baths. An open-air field of ruins.

title: Trier Amphitheater
excerpt: UNESCO-listed amphitheater with underground lift shafts. In summer it hosts the Antikenfestspiele (ancient world festival).

title: Roman Bridge (Römerbrücke)
excerpt: The oldest bridge in Germany north of the Alps. Its Roman piers date to 144 AD.

title: Igel Column (Igeler Säule)
excerpt: A Roman funerary column in the Trier suburb of Igel. 23 m tall and richly decorated.

title: Hauptmarkt (the main market square) with Market Cross
excerpt: Trier's market square since 958. The Steipe, Petrusbrunnen fountain, Market Cross, and Rotes Haus all stand here.

title: Electoral Palace (Kurfürstliches Palais)
excerpt: A pink Rococo palace between the Basilica of Constantine and the palace gardens. One of the finest Rococo palaces in Germany.

title: Dreikönigenhaus (House of the Three Magi)
excerpt: A medieval residential tower on the Simeonstrasse. Entrance on the first floor, a typical defensive house.

title: Rheinisches Landesmuseum (Rhineland State Museum)
excerpt: One of Europe's most important museums of antiquity. Mosaics, gold coins, and the model of Roman Trier.

title: Stadtmuseum Simeonstift (Simeonstift City Museum)
excerpt: City history right next to the Porta Nigra. Includes a Romanesque cloister.

title: Museum am Dom (Cathedral Museum)
excerpt: The cathedral treasury, early Christian finds, and a calm curatorial setting next to the cathedral.

title: Karl Marx House
excerpt: Karl Marx's birthplace (1818). Permanent exhibition on his life, work, and influence.

title: Trier Toy Museum (Spielzeugmuseum)
excerpt: Three floors of toys from 1900 to today. A family favorite right on the Hauptmarkt.

title: St. Matthias Basilica
excerpt: The only apostle's tomb north of the Alps. An active Benedictine abbey with a Romanesque basilica.

title: St. Paulin
excerpt: A Balthasar Neumann Rococo church built 1734-54. One of the most beautiful Baroque churches on the Middle Rhine.

title: Mariensäule (Marian Column) on the Markusberg
excerpt: A 40 m high column topped with a figure of the Virgin Mary. The plateau offers the best view of Trier's skyline.

title: Petrisberg
excerpt: A ridge east of the city. Viewpoint, playground, and hiking area on the grounds of the former state garden show.

title: Olewig Wine Culture Trail
excerpt: An educational trail through the vineyards of Olewig (a wine village within Trier). 5 km, 14 stations, with rest stops overlooking the Moselle.

title: Moselle Promenade
excerpt: Riverside path between Zurlauben and the Roman Bridge. The best walking stretch, with boat docks and restaurants.

title: Park Plaza Trier
excerpt: Comfortable 4-star hotel on the Kornmarkt, three minutes from the Porta Nigra (the Black Gate) and the cathedral.

title: Mercure Hotel Trier Porta Nigra
excerpt: 4-star hotel right next to the Porta Nigra. The location lives up to the name.

title: Hotel Villa Huegel
excerpt: Boutique hotel in an Art Nouveau villa above the city. Spa, fine-dining level, and great views.

title: Best Western Plus Hotel Trier City
excerpt: Modern 4-star hotel on the edge of the old town. Fresh rooms, breakfast buffet, fitness room.

title: Domhotel Trier
excerpt: Family-run 4-star hotel directly on the Hauptmarkt (the main market square). Classic Trier charm.

title: Hotel Roemischer Kaiser
excerpt: Traditional house right next to the Porta Nigra. Cozy, regional, no frills.

title: Hotel zur Glocke
excerpt: Small city hotel on Glockenstrasse. Personally run, fair prices, central location.

title: Aurelius am Dom
excerpt: Modern boutique hotel right behind the cathedral. Clean lines, fair prices, prime location.

title: Ibis Trier
excerpt: Reliable brand standard at fair prices next to the main train station. Arrive, check in, head into town.

title: Hostel Hille
excerpt: Budget address with dorms and private rooms, five minutes on foot from the Porta Nigra.

title: Moselpark Trier
excerpt: Large 4-star hotel on the Moselle riverbank. Pool, spa, conference rooms, family rooms.

title: Hotel Deutscher Hof
excerpt: Long-established address on the southern edge of the old town. Solid 4-star comfort, restaurant, bar.

title: Hotel Petrisberg
excerpt: Family-run 4-star hotel with Trier's best views. High up, quiet, and wine-friendly.

title: Hotel Eurener Hof
excerpt: Venerable inn in Euren (a district of Trier). Wellness, restaurant, family-friendly.

title: Hotel Casa Chiara
excerpt: Small B&B-style hotel on the Moselle riverbank. Personal, fair, with a fresh breakfast.

title: Weinhaus Becker
excerpt: Boutique wine house with the Michelin-starred Becker's restaurant in Olewig (a wine village within Trier). Cuisine and wine at the highest level.

title: Hotel-Weinhaus Haag
excerpt: Family-run wine house in the heart of Olewig. House wines, a traditional tavern room, and short walks home.

title: Becker's Restaurant
excerpt: Michelin-starred restaurant run by the Becker family in Olewig (a wine village within Trier). Modern cuisine, estate wines, wine-led pairings.

title: Schlemmereule
excerpt: Set in the cathedral cellar on the Domfreihof. Mediterranean accents on a regional foundation.

title: Bagatelle
excerpt: Charming bistro with a French slant on Zuckerbergstraße.

title: Weinstube Kesselstatt
excerpt: Classic wine tavern on Liebfrauenplatz. Regional cooking, Mosel wines, and a wonderful terrace.

title: Zum Domstein
excerpt: Restaurant built over a Roman wine press. Regional cuisine plus historical Roman menus.

title: Kartoffelkiste
excerpt: A Trier classic built around potato dishes. Family-friendly, generous portions, cozy.

title: Pfeffermühle
excerpt: Restaurant with a modern take on Moselle cuisine in the Zurlauben fishermen's quarter.

title: Bei Joh und Otto
excerpt: Young restaurant with modern cuisine, a short menu, and a thoughtful wine selection.

title: Weingut Reverchon
excerpt: Winery with a courtyard wine tavern. Estate wines plus Moselle platters, tarte flambée, and winemaker's bread.

title: Weingut Rummel
excerpt: A classic Olewig Straußwirtschaft (seasonal tavern run by a winery). Estate wine, a simple menu, and a local atmosphere.

title: Weinstube Palais Kesselstatt (Olewig)
excerpt: The wine tavern of Weingut Kesselstatt, with tastings and a small menu.

title: Trierer Weinhaus
excerpt: A local classic in Olewig. Well-curated Mosel wine list and simple regional dishes.

title: Ristorante L'Osteria
excerpt: Italian chain serving oversized pizzas on the Hauptmarkt. Reliable and family-friendly.

title: Ristorante La Bottega
excerpt: Ambitious Italian on Glockenstraße. Seasonal menu and a good wine list.

title: Pizzeria Donna Margherita
excerpt: Small pizzeria on Sichelstraße. Wood-fired Neapolitan pizza at fair prices.

title: Vapiano Trier
excerpt: Italian chain with live-cooking stations on the Viehmarkt.

title: Akito Sushi
excerpt: Sushi spot on Bruchhausenstraße. Fresh fish, fair prices, delivery available.

title: Bambus
excerpt: Small Vietnamese restaurant. Pho, bun bo nam bo, and summer rolls.

title: Saigon
excerpt: Thai cuisine on Brückenstraße. Curries, glass noodles, and heat to order.

title: Bistro Quo Vadis
excerpt: Bistro with a rotating daily menu and a solid wine list on Stockplatz.

title: Lieblingsstube
excerpt: Café-bistro serving breakfast all day. Homemade cakes and a small hot menu.

title: Burgerlich
excerpt: Burger spot offering several patty options, a fries menu, and craft beer.

title: Tabula Rasa
excerpt: Young, modern restaurant with rotating menus and bold flavors.

title: Weinstube Friedrich-Wilhelm-Gymnasium
excerpt: The foundation-run wine tavern of the FWG school. Wines from estate vineyards in a classic Trier setting.

title: Innenstadt (Old Town)
excerpt: The ancient heart of Trier, stretching from the Porta Nigra to the Hauptmarkt and the Cathedral. UNESCO World Heritage at every step.

title: Olewig
excerpt: A wine village within the city limits. Family wineries, the Weinkulturpfad (wine culture trail), and the gateway to the Mosel region.

title: Trier-Süd
excerpt: South of the city center. Home to the Kaiserthermen, Barbarathermen, and the Basilica of St. Matthias.

title: Trier-Nord
excerpt: A residential district north of the Porta Nigra. River port, main train station, and the Nordallee.

title: Trier-West / Pallien
excerpt: The left bank of the Moselle. The Mariensäule (Mary Column), the Markusberg hill, and panoramic views of the old town.

title: Trier-Ost
excerpt: Gartenfeld and Heiligkreuz. Residential districts just east of the city center.

title: Mariahof
excerpt: A plateau residential district with wide views and easy access to green space.

title: Kürenz
excerpt: Trier's university quarter, taking in the Petrisberg, the Tarforster Höhe, and a lively student scene.

title: Euren
excerpt: Southwest along the Moselle. A family district close to the Moselle bridge and the Igeler Säule (Igel Column) nearby.

title: Biewer
excerpt: Trier's northernmost Moselle district, close to the river port and steeped in wine tradition.

title: Ruwer-Eitelsbach
excerpt: Trier's eastern reach along the Ruwer valley. A wine village known for famously steep vineyard slopes.

title: Pfalzel
excerpt: A historic village quarter with the remains of a late-Roman royal palace on the Moselle.

title: UNESCO World Heritage
excerpt: Trier's eight UNESCO sites: Roman monuments, the cathedral, and the Liebfrauenkirche.

title: Roman Heritage
excerpt: Porta Nigra, the Imperial Baths, the amphitheater, the Roman Bridge: the old Augusta Treverorum.

title: Wine & the Moselle
excerpt: Olewig, Ruwer, and the Moselle: wineries, wine hikes, wine festivals.

title: Museums
excerpt: Rheinisches Landesmuseum, Stadtmuseum Simeonstift, and the Karl-Marx-Haus.

title: Churches & Pilgrimage Sites
excerpt: Cathedral, Liebfrauenkirche, Konstantinbasilika, St. Matthias, St. Paulin.

title: Trier with Kids
excerpt: Amphitheater tours, the toy museum, the Roman-themed bus, and the Petrisberg.

title: Food & Drink
excerpt: Moselle wine taverns, fine dining, and wine-house restaurants in Olewig.

title: Views & Hiking
excerpt: Mariensäule, Petrisberg, the Olewig Wine Trail, and the Moselle promenade.

title: Moselle Activities
excerpt: The Moselle cycle path, river cruises, and passenger boats to Bernkastel.

title: Shopping
excerpt: Simeonstraße, Fleischstraße, Brotstraße: Trier's pedestrian zone.

title: 3 Days in Trier: the UNESCO Itinerary
excerpt: Itinerary: 3 days in Trier with UNESCO World Heritage sites, the Moselle promenade, Olewig, and practical tips.

title: Trier with Kids
excerpt: Itinerary: Trier with kids. Gladiator tour at the Amphitheater, toy museum, Petrisberg, bus tour, and ice cream on the Hauptmarkt.

title: Trier in the Rain
excerpt: Itinerary: Trier in the rain. UNESCO must-sees indoors, three museums, cafe stops, and traditional wine bars.

title: Trier Antikenfestspiele 2026 (Classical Antiquity Festival)
excerpt: The Antikenfestspiele turn the Roman amphitheater into Germany's largest open-air ancient-world stage every summer. Stagings with live music, horse riders, and thousands of spectators under the open sky.

title: Olewiger Weinfest 2026 (Olewig Wine Festival)
excerpt: The largest wine festival in the region. Seven days of Riesling and Elbling, open winery courtyards, the parade on Sunday, and the crowning of the Trier Wine Queen.

title: Trier Weihnachtsmarkt 2026 (Christmas Market)
excerpt: One of Germany's most beautiful Christmas markets. Stalls on the Hauptmarkt (the main market square) and in front of the cathedral, a medieval backdrop, mulled wine, and a Roman-flavored Christmas atmosphere.

title: Trier Altstadtfest 2026 (Old Town Festival)
excerpt: Three days of city festival with stages on the Hauptmarkt, Kornmarkt, and by the cathedral. Live music, food and drink, and free guided city tours.

title: Brot und Spiele 2026 (Bread and Circuses Roman Festival)
excerpt: Trier goes Roman for a weekend. Gladiator combat in the amphitheater, a legionary camp, ancient crafts, and a costume parade.

title: Mosel-Wein-Nachts-Bummel 2026 (Moselle Wine Night Stroll)
excerpt: A summer night along Trier's Moselle promenade. Regional wine stalls, live music, and light art along the riverside walk.

title: Trier Lange Nacht der Museen 2026 (Long Night of Museums)
excerpt: One night, every museum open. From the Rheinisches Landesmuseum (Regional Archaeological Museum) and the Karl-Marx-Haus (Karl Marx's birthplace) to the Spielzeugmuseum (Toy Museum). Shuttle bus included.

title: Wine Festivals in Trier and Along the Moselle: The Complete Guide
excerpt: Olewig Wine Festival, Wine in the City, Bernkastel and more: all the key wine festivals in Trier and along the Moselle, with tips on season, atmosphere, and how to get there.

title: Riesling Routes Along the Moselle from Trier
excerpt: The most scenic Riesling routes from Trier: wine villages, steep vineyards, tastings and tours along the Moselle from Trier to Koblenz at a glance.

title: Sparkling Wine Cellars in Trier: Bubbly Experiences Between the Moselle and the Romans
excerpt: Explore Trier's sparkling wine cellars: Bernard-Massard, traditional bottle fermentation, cellar tours and tastings in Germany's oldest city.

title: The Best Vinotheques in Trier: Where Mosel Wine Becomes an Experience
excerpt: The finest wine bars and vinotheques in and around Trier: where to taste, buy, and enjoy Riesling from the Mosel, Saar, and Ruwer rivers.

title: Wine Tastings in Trier's Historic Cellars
excerpt: Wine tastings in Trier's historic cellars: Roman origins, Riesling from the Mosel, Saar, and Ruwer, guided cellar tours, and tips for the perfect experience.

title: Mosel Wine Tours in Trier: Tastings, Winery Visits and Experiences
excerpt: Mosel wine tours in Trier: tastings in the Old Town, guided winery walks along the Weinkulturpfad, river cruises and wine in the dark. Tips for wine lovers.

title: Wine Taverns in Trier: The Best Spots for Mosel Wine
excerpt: The best wine taverns (Weinstuben) in Trier: relaxed addresses for Riesling, tapas and regional cuisine between the city center and Olewig. Tips for your wine night out.

title: Wine Tours by Bike Along the Moselle Near Trier
excerpt: Cycling through the Moselle vineyards near Trier: the flat Moselle Cycle Route, family wineries, Riesling tastings, and tips for a relaxed tour.

title: Wine Seminars in Trier: Knowledge and Enjoyment of Mosel Wine
excerpt: Wine seminars in Trier: learn to taste, understand Riesling and Elbling, try wine and chocolate pairings, and join guided vineyard tours along the Moselle.

title: Wine Traditions in Trier: 2,000 Years of Wine Culture on the Moselle
excerpt: From the Romans to today: how Trier's wine traditions developed over two millennia, and why the city is regarded as Germany's oldest wine town.

title: Wine Hotels in Trier: Staying Among the Vineyards on the Moselle
excerpt: Wine hotels in Trier and along the Moselle: romantic getaways, family stays, wellness retreats and overnights with vineyard views. Your overview for travelers who come for the food and wine.

title: Wine Hikes in Trier and Along the Moselle: The Most Scenic Routes Through the Vineyards
excerpt: Wine hikes around Trier: the Weinkulturpfad in Olewig, the Ruwer Riesling trail, Trittenheim and the Middle Moselle. Routes, walking times and tips for hikers who like to taste as they go.

title: Winery Tours in Trier: A Look Behind the Scenes with Moselle Vintners
excerpt: Winery tours in Trier and along the Moselle: wine cellars, tastings, sustainable viticulture and practical tips on duration, booking and grape varieties.

title: Organic Wineries in Trier and Along the Moselle: Sustainable Wine Country
excerpt: Organic wineries around Trier and along the Moselle: cultivation methods, Riesling from steep slate slopes, certifications like Ecovin and Demeter, plus tips for tastings.

title: Wineries in Trier: A Guide to Vineyards, Estates, and Grape Varieties
excerpt: An overview of wineries in Trier: history, leading estates, the vineyard regions along the Moselle, Saar, and Ruwer, plus grape varieties and experiences for wine lovers.
